metadata.description: Cross-species pathway comparison database for translational medicine

pathways.name: Retinoblastoma (Rb) Tumor Suppressor Pathway
pathways.description: Controls G1/S cell cycle transition. Dysregulated in virtually all human cancers.
pathways.disease_relevance: Retinoblastoma, osteosarcoma, SCLC, breast cancer, bladder cancer

components.function: Binds and represses E2F transcription factors. Core brake on cell cycle entry.

mouse.note: KO lethal E14.5; het → pituitary tumours

drosophila.note: Primary fly homolog; loss → ectopic S-phase + apoptosis

human_mouse.notes: 97% protein identity; functionally interchangeable in most contexts

human_drosophila.notes: Pocket domain conserved; overall 52% identity; 2 fly paralogs vs 3 human

human_worm.notes: Single C. elegans homolog; pocket domain recognisable but diverged

components.translational_notes: Core pocket domain highly conserved. Mouse models predictive. Fly models valid for pathway logic but paralog number differs.

components.function: Phosphorylates and inactivates Rb. Activated by Cyclin D.

human.disease: Amplified in liposarcoma, breast; CDK4-R24C in familial melanoma

mouse.note: Cdk4-R24C KI → melanoma + pituitary; Cdk6 dominant in lymphoid cells

drosophila.note: Single Cdk4; no Cdk6 homolog in fly

worm.note: Single homolog; controls G1 in developing vulva

human_mouse.notes: Nearly identical. R24C mutation conserved in function.

human_drosophila.notes: Fly Cdk4 kinase domain well conserved; absence of Cdk6 is key divergence

human_worm.notes: Kinase domain conserved; regulatory context differs

components.translational_notes: CDK4 highly conserved. CDK6 absent in fly — important for haematopoietic models. R24C functional equivalence validated across species.

components.function: Activates CDK4/6 in response to mitogenic signals. Three mammalian isoforms with tissue-specific expression.

human.disease: CCND1 amplified in breast, mantle cell lymphoma, head & neck

mouse.note: Ccnd1 OE → mammary adenocarcinoma; tissue isoforms conserved

drosophila.note: Single isoform; less mitogenic role; more involved in growth/endoreplication

zebrafish.note: 3 isoforms present; ccnd1 expressed in brain development

worm.note: Single homolog; essential for G1 progression

human_mouse.notes: Isoform pattern and tissue specificity conserved

human_zebrafish.notes: 3 isoforms present; ccnd1 brain role conserved

human_drosophila.notes: Single fly CycD; functional emphasis differs — more growth than cell cycle entry

components.translational_notes: Fly CycD is functionally diverged — less predictive for cell cycle entry models. Mouse and zebrafish adequate. Key isoform distinction: CCND1 (breast/GI), CCND2 (haematopoietic), CCND3 (lymphoid).

components.function: Binds CDK4/6 monomers, prevents Cyclin D association, maintains Rb in active state.

human.disease: CDKN2A most commonly silenced gene in cancer; familial melanoma, pancreatic, NSCLC

mouse.note: All 4 present; Cdkn2a KO → lymphomas/sarcomas; p19ARF from same locus

drosophila.note: CRITICAL GAP — fly has no INK4 family member; Roughex (Rux) is partial functional analog only

zebrafish.note: p16 and p15 present; p18/p19 less clear; zebrafish p16 regulates melanocyte senescence

human_mouse.notes: All 4 members present. p19ARF (mouse) ≈ p14ARF (human) — same locus, ~50% sequence identity, equivalent function

human_zebrafish.notes: p16/p15 conserved; p18/p19 diverged; zebrafish p16 validated in melanoma modelling

human_drosophila.notes: No INK4 homolog. Roughex (Rux) overlaps functionally but is not structurally related. MAJOR translational gap.

human_worm.notes: No INK4 homolog in C. elegans

components.translational_notes: CRITICAL: Fly is a poor model for INK4-mediated CDK4/6 inhibition (e.g. palbociclib MOA). Mouse and zebrafish are appropriate. Any drug screen in Drosophila that depends on p16 function will not translate.

components.function: Drives S-phase gene transcription when released from Rb. E2F1 also activates ARF/apoptosis.

human.disease: E2F1 overexpression in many cancers; E2F3 amplified in bladder cancer

mouse.note: E2f1 KO → testicular atrophy + T-cell lymphoma; closely mirrors human

drosophila.note: Clean activator/repressor split — informed human E2F biology; 2 members vs 8

zebrafish.note: Full E2F family present; e2f1 regulates eye/brain development

worm.note: 2 homologs; efl-1 is activating, efl-2 repressive — mirrors fly

human_mouse.notes: 8-member family conserved; tissue and cell-cycle-stage expression patterns equivalent

human_zebrafish.notes: Full family present; key regulatory roles in development conserved

human_drosophila.notes: Core DNA-binding domain conserved; only 2 members. Activator/repressor logic intact. Good model for pathway logic, not isoform-specific biology.

human_worm.notes: efl-1/efl-2 mirror the activator/repressor split; core function conserved in simpler context

components.translational_notes: E2F transcription is a highly conserved node. Fly useful for pathway-level screens. For isoform-specific biology (E2F3 in bladder cancer, E2F4 in quiescence), mouse is required.

components.function: Broadly inhibits Cyclin-CDK complexes (especially Cyclin E-CDK2). Induced by DNA damage (p21) and growth factor withdrawal (p27).

human.disease: p27 loss correlates with poor prognosis breast/colorectal; p57 mutations in Beckwith-Wiedemann

mouse.note: p27 KO → pituitary hyperplasia; p57 essential for placental development

drosophila.note: Single CIP/KIP homolog; most similar to p27; essential for G1 in post-mitotic cells

zebrafish.note: p21/p27 conserved; p21 induced by DNA damage; p57 role in somitogenesis

worm.note: 2 members; cki-1 controls G1 arrest in development

human_mouse.notes: All 3 members conserved; p21 p53-responsiveness equivalent

human_drosophila.notes: Single Dacapo most similar to p27. p21 DNA damage response not equivalent. Reduced complexity.

human_worm.notes: cki-1/cki-2 functional analogs; less sequence identity but conserved CDK-inhibitory role

components.translational_notes: Dacapo (fly) covers p27-like function but NOT p21 DNA damage response — important gap for genotoxic drug studies. Mouse covers all three members fully.

components.function: ARF sequesters MDM2/Mdm2, stabilising p53. Links Rb loss to apoptosis/senescence. Critical secondary checkpoint.

human.disease: TP53 mutated in >50% cancers; MDM2 amplified in sarcomas

mouse.note: p19ARF ≈ p14ARF; ~50% sequence identity but equivalent Mdm2 sequestration

drosophila.note: p53 homolog present but E2F→ARF→MDM2 link is weaker; no clear ARF homolog

zebrafish.note: p53/mdm2 axis well conserved; zebrafish p53 mutants are tumour prone

worm.note: cep-1 regulates apoptosis in germline; less clear somatic tumour suppressor role

human_mouse.notes: Functionally equivalent; p19ARF/p14ARF are different isoforms of same logic. Co-deletion of Rb+p53 required for full escape.

human_zebrafish.notes: p53/mdm2 axis conserved; ARF less clear

human_drosophila.notes: Dp53 present but ARF-MDM2 link is partial. Fly apoptosis program diverged (uses Reaper/Hid/Grim). Important gap for p53-targeted drug studies.

human_worm.notes: cep-1 is germline apoptosis regulator; somatic tumour suppressor axis less conserved

components.translational_notes: IMPORTANT: Fly is a poor model for ARF-p53-MDM2 axis. MDM2 inhibitors (nutlin-3a) should be validated in mouse or zebrafish, not fly. Worm unsuitable for this axis.

pathways.name: Notch Signalling Pathway
pathways.description: Cell-cell communication pathway controlling cell fate, differentiation, and tissue homeostasis.
pathways.disease_relevance: T-ALL, CADASIL, Alagille syndrome, colorectal cancer, breast cancer

components.function: Receives Delta/Jagged ligand signals. Cleaved to release NICD which activates target genes.

human.disease: NOTCH1 mutations in T-ALL (>50%); NOTCH3 in CADASIL

mouse.note: Notch1 KO lethal; Notch2 KO → vascular defects; closely mirrors human

drosophila.note: Single gene — where Notch was DISCOVERED; wing notching phenotype. Founding member.

zebrafish.note: Multiple paralogs from teleost duplication; notch signalling controls somitogenesis clock

worm.note: lin-12 controls vulval fate; glp-1 controls germline proliferation

human_mouse.notes: All 4 paralogs conserved with equivalent tissue expression

human_zebrafish.notes: Conserved; teleost duplication adds paralogs but function maintained

human_drosophila.notes: Core EGF repeats and NICD domain conserved. 1 fly gene vs 4 human. Founding discovery context.

human_worm.notes: lin-12/glp-1 functional conservation; used extensively to define Notch logic

components.translational_notes: Notch pathway logic deeply conserved. Fly models excellent for ligand-receptor interaction and downstream CSL/RBPJ signalling. Mouse required for tissue-specific isoform studies (NOTCH3 in vasculature).

pathways.name: Wnt / β-catenin Signalling Pathway
pathways.description: Regulates cell proliferation, differentiation, and tissue homeostasis. Named after Drosophila wingless (wg).
pathways.disease_relevance: Colorectal cancer (APC), hepatocellular carcinoma, Wnt-driven medulloblastoma, developmental syndromes

components.function: Binds Frizzled/LRP co-receptor to activate β-catenin pathway.

human.disease: WNT5A in melanoma; WNT signalling in >90% CRC

mouse.note: Wnt1 KO → cerebellum defects; mammary-specific Wnt1 OE → adenocarcinoma

drosophila.note: wingless (wg) is the founding member; 7 fly Wnt genes; wg controls segment polarity

zebrafish.note: Many paralogs from teleost duplication; wnt signalling controls axis formation

worm.note: 5 Wnt genes; control cell polarity and fate specification

human_mouse.notes: 19-member family conserved; tissue expression patterns equivalent

human_zebrafish.notes: High conservation; teleost paralogs from WGD

human_drosophila.notes: wingless founding member; 7 fly Wnt genes. Core pathway logic (β-catenin/Armadillo) highly conserved.

human_worm.notes: 5 worm Wnt genes; pathway logic conserved but regulatory context diverged

components.translational_notes: Wnt ligand specificity varies by tissue. Fly excellent for canonical Wnt/Wg pathway logic but ligand-receptor specificity differs. Mouse required for tissue-specific Wnt isoform studies.

components.function: Part of β-catenin destruction complex with Axin and GSK3β. APC loss → β-catenin nuclear accumulation → oncogenic Wnt target activation.

human.disease: FAP syndrome; mutated in >80% sporadic colorectal cancer

mouse.note: Apc^Min/+ mouse — gold standard CRC model; develops intestinal polyps

drosophila.note: 2 fly APC genes; Apc2 (E-APC) more important in Wg signalling; well conserved

zebrafish.note: apc mutants show CNS overgrowth; intestinal phenotype less clear

worm.note: apr-1 controls cell polarity; partial functional conservation

human_mouse.notes: Highly conserved. Apc^Min/+ mouse is validated translational model for CRC.

human_zebrafish.notes: apc conserved; intestinal model less developed than mouse

human_drosophila.notes: 2 fly APC genes; armadillo (β-catenin) destruction complex conserved. Fly useful for APC-Wnt pathway screens.

human_worm.notes: apr-1 functional overlap; not a strong CRC model

components.translational_notes: APC function in Wnt/β-catenin destruction complex is well conserved. Fly Apc/Apc2 suitable for Wnt pathway drug screens. Apc^Min/+ mouse is validated CRC model — use for therapeutic validation.
